metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve 'git worktree / temp checkout' to ONE mechanism and document its setup/teardown contract (e.g., if git worktree: specify whether a bare repo per project is pre-cloned under checkout_root; if temp clone: clarify that checkout_root is just a scratch parent dir).
findings.finding: The event-delivery section specifies the per-revision checkout mechanism as 'a git worktree / temp checkout' — two distinct implementation approaches (git-worktree requires a pre-existing main repo under checkout_root; a temp clone does not), with different setup, teardown, and failure semantics. An implementer cannot determine which to use from the plan text alone.
findings.location: Event delivery + repo_root acquisition section — 'the handler materializes a per-revision ephemeral checkout (a git worktree / temp checkout keyed by revision_id)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit default or 'required (no default)' annotation for checkout_root in the [gerrit_voter] config schema, consistent with how all other fields in that section document their defaults.
findings.finding: The `[gerrit_voter]` config section specifies explicit defaults for enabled (false), enabled_projects ([]), max_vote (1), and label ('LLM-Review') but gives NO default for checkout_root — unlike other fields. It is unclear whether checkout_root is a required field (server fails to start without it) or has an implicit default (e.g., /tmp/rebar-gerrit-checkouts), and this gap affects error-handling and deployment documentation.
findings.location: Staged opt-in + rollback section — checkout_root config field definition
findings.suggested_fix: If a sensible default exists (e.g., `/tmp/rebar-gerrit-checkouts`), add `checkout_root: str = "/tmp/rebar-gerrit-checkouts"` to the config table; otherwise mark it explicitly as required with no default and add startup validation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the 'see AC7' reference in the What section to 'see AC9' (or whichever numbered position the ADR criterion occupies) so the cross-reference matches the actual criterion.
findings.finding: The 'Why' / 'What' section says 'ADR 0013 capturing the voter architecture (see AC7)' but the ADR criterion is the 9th bullet in the Acceptance Criteria list (AC9 by count), not AC7. AC7 covers the diff-fetch failure behavior. This internal cross-reference points to the wrong criterion.
findings.location: What section: 'ADR 0013 capturing the voter architecture (see AC7)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'ADR 0013 capturing the voter architecture (see AC7)' → 'ADR 0013 capturing the voter architecture (see AC9)' in the What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC4's criterion text to explicitly include `checkout_root` (base path for per-revision checkouts) and `label` (Gerrit label name, config not hardcoded) so all described `[gerrit_voter]` config fields have a covering criterion.
findings.finding: AC4's verification text enumerates only three `[gerrit_voter]` fields (`enabled`, `enabled_projects`, `max_vote`) but the plan body describes two additional config fields — `checkout_root` and `label` — as part of the same config section. These described deliverables have no covering acceptance criterion.
findings.location: AC4 (Staged opt-in criterion) vs. 'Staged opt-in + rollback' section and 'Event delivery + repo_root acquisition' section
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC4: '…; `label` (Gerrit label name, not hardcoded); `checkout_root` (base path for per-revision ephemeral checkouts); config schema test asserts all fields with their defaults.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the per-revision ephemeral checkout is cleaned up via a try/finally (or equivalent context-manager) that runs on ALL exit paths — happy-path vote, VoteError, diff-fetch failure, and uncaught exception — and add a stub-test assertion that the worktree is removed even when the vote POST fails.
findings.finding: The ephemeral git worktree created per revision has no guaranteed cleanup in error paths. The plan states the checkout is 'removed after voting,' but the VoteError path (vote POST fails), the diff-fetch-failure path (handler posts -1 without calling the adapter), and any uncaught exception between worktree creation and the cleanup step could all leave worktrees accumulating indefinitely under `gerrit_voter.checkout_root`.
findings.location: Event delivery + `repo_root` acquisition section; AC3 (stub end-to-end); REST vote failure behavior section
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the worktree lifecycle in a context manager (`with ephemeral_checkout(revision_id, checkout_root) as repo_root:`) so cleanup is guaranteed regardless of exception type or early-return path; document this in the 'Event delivery + repo_root acquisition' section and add it to AC3's stub-test assertions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider splitting into two leaves: (a) adapter seam + vote-mapping + stub harness foundation (AC1, AC2, AC5, AC8 — pure logic, no HTTP); (b) Gerrit REST client + patchset handler + diff-fetch failure + config opt-in (AC3, AC4, AC6, AC7, AC9) — this preserves the vertical-slice sequencing, de-risks the REST/network boundary separately, and keeps each leaf under 5 ACs.
findings.finding: The leaf ticket carries 9 Acceptance Criteria and introduces ≥3 independent components (adapter seam, Gerrit REST client, patchset handler + config/opt-in module), each independently deliverable and testable. The rubric flags >6 criteria on a leaf as a decomposition signal; the 'shared stub harness' rationale is stated but not uniquely blocking — the stub harness is itself reusable across child tickets.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria section (AC1–AC9, 9 total); 'What' section listing adapter/REST client/handler/config as four distinct deliverables
findings.suggested_fix: Split leaf into WS6-a-i (adapter seam: AC1, AC2, AC5, AC8 — adapter logic + vote mapping + message vocabulary + force-enable, ~100 LOC, no HTTP) and WS6-a-ii (Gerrit integration: AC3, AC4, AC6, AC7, AC9 — REST client + handler + config + ADR, ~250 LOC). WS6-a-ii depends on WS6-a-i. The stub harness is initialized in WS6-a-i and extended in WS6-a-ii.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a normalization step in the diff-fetch path: after base64-decoding the `/patch` response, strip the `git format-patch` RFC-2822 envelope (headers up to the first `diff --git` line) to obtain a bare unified diff before passing to `diff_text`; alternatively, use the `/files` endpoint to derive `changed_files` and fetch the raw diff via a different mechanism (e.g., the Gerrit `diff` endpoint per file, or request `format=TEXT` where available).
findings.finding: The Gerrit `GET /a/changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/patch` endpoint returns a base64-encoded `git format-patch` blob (RFC-2822 email format: `From <sha>`, `Date:`, `Subject:` headers preceding the diff hunks), NOT a raw `git diff -U` unified diff. The plan says 'decode to `diff_text`' with no normalization step. The existing `assemble_diff_context(diff_text=...)` was built against `git diff` output; feeding it format-patch output will produce malformed diff parsing (misidentified file paths, incorrect hunk boundaries, potentially empty changed_files lists).
findings.location: Gerrit REST API surface section — 'Diff fetch' bullet; AC3
findings.suggested_fix: In the patchset handler, after base64-decoding the `/patch` response, apply a normalization function: `diff_text = re.sub(r'^.*?(?=^diff --git)', '', raw, count=1, flags=re.DOTALL|re.MULTILINE)` to strip the format-patch envelope. Document this in the REST surface section and add a stub test that supplies a format-patch-shaped blob and asserts correct changed_files extraction.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten the `coverage_gap` derivation to filter `security_detectors` notes by `blocking==True`: `any(n.get('reason') == 'fail-closed-abstain' and n.get('blocking', True) for n in coverage.get('security_detectors', []))`, so advisory-only abstains do not trigger a fail-closed -1.
findings.finding: The `coverage_gap` derivation (`any(n.get('reason') == 'fail-closed-abstain' for n in coverage.get('security_detectors', []))`) does not filter by `n.get('blocking') == True`. In `apply_failclosed`, a note with `reason='fail-closed-abstain'` is appended even when `blocking_enabled=False` for that criterion — but in that case `verdict['verdict']` is NOT flipped to BLOCK. So if a detector criterion has `blocking_enabled=False` and abstains, the adapter would see `coverage_gap=True` → emit decision=BLOCK → post `-1`, even though the abstain is advisory-only and the LLM pass may have returned PASS. This incorrectly vetoes changes due to a non-blocking advisory abstain.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the security_detectors sub-expression with: `any(n.get('reason') == 'fail-closed-abstain' and n.get('blocking', True) for n in coverage.get('security_detectors', []))`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the ephemeral worktree is managed in a `try/finally` block (or equivalent context manager) so cleanup runs on both the happy path and exception/error paths; also specify stale-worktree detection on startup or before `git worktree add` (e.g., `git worktree remove --force` if the path already exists).
findings.finding: The per-revision ephemeral git worktree is specified to be 'removed after voting,' but the plan does not address cleanup on the exception/error path (e.g., an unexpected exception from `produce_code_review_verdict`, a process kill during the gate run, or an unhandled error before the cleanup call). A leaked worktree per failed event accumulates on disk under `checkout_root`, and `git worktree` state is not self-cleaning.
findings.location: Event delivery + repo_root acquisition section; AC3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact field and format used to embed the scanner identity in the `[LLM-Review: BLOCK — coverage-gap (scanner)]` message — e.g., 'use `note["criterion"]` for each abstaining detector note, joined by `", "` if multiple' — so AC5's test can be written unambiguously.
findings.finding: The 'scanner' coverage-gap sub-reason in the review-message vocabulary says 'the message names which scanner,' but no rule specifies WHICH field from the `security_detectors` note structure (criterion: 'secret-detection'/'high-critical-security', reason: 'fail-closed-abstain', abstain_reasons: [...], blocking: bool — per detectors.py:96–115) is used to populate the scanner identity in the message string. An implementor cannot unambiguously construct this message without knowing whether to embed the `criterion` value, a human-readable label, or the `abstain_reasons` contents.
findings.location: Review-message vocabulary section / AC5 (`[LLM-Review: BLOCK — coverage-gap (scanner)]` tag description)
findings.suggested_fix: In the review-message vocabulary section, add: 'For the `scanner` sub-reason, the scanner identity is `note["criterion"]` from each `coverage["security_detectors"]` note where `note["reason"] == "fail-closed-abstain"`, joined by ", " if multiple (e.g., `coverage-gap (scanner: secret-detection)`).' This directly maps to the existing detectors.py note structure.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
